extraction.one_line_summary: SAGAN Peter attacked 53km from finish at Mons-en-Pévèle cobbles, established 1:30 lead, held off TERPSTRA Niki's chase to win alone.
tactical_patterns: SAGAN Peter attacked from favorites group rather than bridging to breakaway first, avoiding energy expenditure on chase.
TERPSTRA Niki drove chase group at Carrefour de l'Arbre but started organizational work too late to close 1:30 gap.
Bora-Hansgrohe shut down TEUNISSEN Mike/GILBERT Philippe/POLITT Nils trio early, forcing SAGAN Peter to attack from larger group later.
DILLIER Silvan track background insufficient for velodrome sprint against road power, suggesting track pedigree overrated in Roubaix finish.

rider_signals.SAGAN Peter: positive: Won after mechanical self-repair suggests exceptional form when maintaining race-winning gap requires no team support.
rider_signals.DILLIER Silvan: positive: Only rider matching SAGAN's tempo from 20km suggests Swiss climbers undervalued on sustained cobbled power sections.
rider_signals.TERPSTRA Niki: neutral: Led chase to third but organizational work began too late, suggesting team isolation or tactical miscalculation.
rider_signals.PHINNEY Taylor: positive: Eighth place in reduced sprint group indicates American time-trialists handle cobbled attrition better than positioning suggests.
rider_signals.GILBERT Philippe: negative: Early attack at section 19 with TEUNISSEN neutralized, lacked legs to follow when SAGAN launched decisive move.
